# CSMP Gate (c) — Survivorship / Point-in-Time Universe Membership Audit

*Generated by `scripts/csmp/audit_universe.py` — every number below is derived from the store and reproducible on re-run.*

- **Store:** 4,099 trade dates, 7,030,920 rows (bit-unmodified since gate (a))
- **Universe:** 35,000 member-cells across 175 monthly rebalances (2012-01 → present), `method = turnover_top200`
- **Rule (charter D1, locked):** top 200 equities by 6-month median daily turnover; rebalance = last full session (`n_symbols ≥ 200`) of each month; eligibility floor = traded on ≥ 60% of the lookback's full sessions. No parameter here was grid-searched.

## 1. Source Decision

The charter prefers true point-in-time NIFTY-200 index membership if obtainable. **It is not obtainable as a point-in-time change history.** Each probe is recorded in `universe_probes`; the salient outcomes:

| Probe | URL | Outcome | Verdict |
|-------|-----|---------|---------|
| current_list_validation_only | `https://archives.nseindia.com/content/indices/ind_nifty200list.csv` | 200 200 symbols | survivor_current |
| historical_change_history | `https://archives.nseindia.com/content/indices/historical/IndexConstituentHistory.xlsx` | HTTP 404 len=160 | absent |
| instrument_master | `https://nsearchives.nseindia.com/content/equities/EQUITY_L.csv` | cached | local |
| niftyindices_csv_api | `https://www.niftyindices.com/IndexConstituent/ind_Nifty200.csv` | 200 len=77360 wrong-content(HTML) | survivor_current_or_unreachable |

The `niftyindices` CSV API answers a **wrong-content 200** — an HTML shell, not the constituent CSV (gate-a's G4 failure mode: NSE serves wrong-content 200s). No dated add/drop change-history file is published. The archives carry only the **current** survivor list, which is exactly the bias this gate exists to prevent: *a present-day constituent list validates a reconstruction, it is not a source of one.*

**Decision:** the mechanical top-200-by-turnover rule — the charter-locked fallback — defines membership. The current NSE list is fetched and used as a **validation cross-check only** (section 4), never as a membership input. The rule's parameters are stated, not tuned:

- **Rebalance date:** last full session (`n_symbols ≥ 200`) of each calendar month, 2012-01 → present. Dates are not hand-picked.
- **Lookback:** the 6 calendar months through and including the rebalance month (≈126 sessions). Runway: gate (a) ingests from 2010-01.
- **Metric:** median of daily turnover over the lookback (median, not mean — robust to a single block-deal spike). **Raw** turnover: a split conserves price×quantity, so turnover needs no corporate-action adjustment and gate (b)'s adjusted view is not consulted. Gate (a) confirms `turnover` is populated for the whole span (0 NULLs); no span falls back to close×volume.
- **Eligibility floor:** traded on ≥ 60% of the lookback's full sessions (a listing/liquidity floor — a name listed two weeks has no 6-month turnover).
- **Membership:** the top 200 eligible equities; if fewer than 200 exist on a date, all eligible are taken and the shortfall disclosed — never padded to 200.

**Membership turnover (adds/drops per rebalance) — plausibility check.** A top-200-by-liquidity universe should churn a handful of names per month, not half the book.

- Median adds/rebalance: **6** | median drops/rebalance: **6**
- Highest-churn rebalance: 2017-11-30 (+12 / −12)
| Rebalance | Adds | Drops |

## 3. Point-in-Time / No-Leak Proof

**The test is code and runs in this report.** For a sample of rebalances spanning all years, membership on `t` is recomputed from a store **logically truncated at `t`** (every query filtered `trade_date ≤ t`) and asserted byte-identical to the full-store membership recorded for `t`. Any dependence on a future row — a symbol ranked on turnover it only earned after `t`, or admitted because it survived to today — makes the two differ and fails the run loudly.

| Rebalance date | Full-store members | Truncated-≤t members | Identical? |
|----------------|-------------------:|---------------------:|:----------:|
| 2012-01-31 | 200 | 200 | **YES** |
| 2013-01-31 | 200 | 200 | **YES** |
| 2014-01-31 | 200 | 200 | **YES** |
| 2015-01-30 | 200 | 200 | **YES** |
| 2016-01-29 | 200 | 200 | **YES** |
| 2017-01-31 | 200 | 200 | **YES** |
| 2018-01-31 | 200 | 200 | **YES** |
| 2019-01-31 | 200 | 200 | **YES** |
| 2020-01-31 | 200 | 200 | **YES** |
| 2021-01-29 | 200 | 200 | **YES** |
| 2022-01-31 | 200 | 200 | **YES** |
| 2023-01-31 | 200 | 200 | **YES** |
| 2024-01-31 | 200 | 200 | **YES** |
| 2025-01-31 | 200 | 200 | **YES** |
| 2026-01-30 | 200 | 200 | **YES** |
| 2026-07-09 | 200 | 200 | **YES** |

**NO-LEAK TEST: PASSED** — all 16 sampled rebalances reproduce byte-identical membership under an independent point-in-time recomputation. The rule is point-in-time by construction (the lookback window lies entirely at or before `t`); `membership_as_of` re-derives membership from a second implementation that filters every source row to `trade_date ≤ t`, and the two agree on every sampled date — a future-information dependency in either path would make them diverge.

*Retention demonstration:* the truncated build **keeps** `INDIABULLS` on 2012-01-31 — a name whose last session was 2013-03-18 (it later stopped trading). A survivor-biased build would have already dropped it; the point-in-time build retains it because only information up to `t` is used.

## 4. Survivorship Proof

No present-day survivor list is a data input to membership. The only external list fetched (today's NIFTY-200) is used here as a **validation cross-check** — to show the build includes names that today's survivor list omits.

A member entity counts as **gone from the panel** when its last recorded session is > 60 days before the store end (2026-07-09) — delisted, merged, suspended, or otherwise no longer trading; 94 such entities were once members. Named examples — each a member for the rebalances it was trading, absent thereafter (unambiguous mergers include HDFC→HDFCBANK, MINDTREE→LTIM, TATACOFFEE→TATACONSUM):

| Symbol | Universe entry | Universe exit | Last session |
|--------|----------------|---------------|--------------|
| PEL | 2012-06-29 | 2025-01-31 | 2025-09-22 |
| IDFC | 2012-01-31 | 2024-01-31 | 2024-10-09 |
| RAJESHEXPO | 2015-08-31 | 2023-11-30 | 2025-12-24 |
| HDFC | 2012-01-31 | 2023-06-30 | 2023-07-12 |
| MINDTREE | 2012-09-28 | 2022-10-31 | 2022-11-22 |
| TV18BRDCST | 2012-12-31 | 2022-09-30 | 2024-10-15 |
| ISEC | 2020-05-29 | 2021-10-29 | 2025-03-21 |
| TATACOFFEE | 2012-01-31 | 2021-10-29 | 2024-01-12 |
| TATASTLBSL | 2012-01-31 | 2021-09-30 | 2021-11-12 |
| BURGERKING | 2021-04-30 | 2021-05-31 | 2022-02-16 |
| DHANI | 2017-06-30 | 2021-04-30 | 2025-10-27 |
| SPICEJET | 2019-12-31 | 2021-04-30 | 2023-04-28 |
| FRETAIL | 2017-08-31 | 2021-01-29 | 2022-10-24 |
| EQUITAS | 2016-08-31 | 2020-12-31 | 2023-02-02 |

(All 94 delisted entities are enumerable from `universe_intervals`, which holds 592 member entities in total.)

Across all rebalances, **2,808** member-cells sit on a name later gone from the panel — names a today's-list-only build could never contain.

**Today's-list omission proof:** of the build's 200 members on the first rebalance (2012-01-31), **112** are absent from today's NIFTY-200 list. Examples (members the build correctly includes that a survivor-biased list would omit): `TATAMOTORS`, `HDFC`, `JPASSOCIAT`, `RELCAPITAL`, `IDFC`, `HDIL`, `STER`, `VIPIND`, `RELINFRA`, `RCOM`, `SESAGOA`, `EDUCOMP`, `CAIRN`, `UNITECH`.

## 5. Eligibility & Non-Equity Exclusion

Equity is the **default**; instruments are excluded only when positively identified as non-equity. Identification uses `symbol_isin` (ISIN primary), the NSE `EQUITY_L` instrument master, gate-(a) H2's `%BEES%/%ETF%/%GOLD%` name pattern as a fallback, and the `-RE` rights-entitlement suffix. ISIN prefixes: `INE*` = company (equity), `INF*` = mutual-fund scheme / ETF, `IN0*`/`IN9*` = government paper / Sovereign Gold Bond.

| Class | Count | Disposition |
|-------|------:|-------------|
| equity_confirmed | 3,313 | eligible (INE ISIN: symbol_isin or EQUITY_L master) |
| non_equity_isin | 315 | excluded (INF*/IN0*/IN9* ISIN) |
| equity_unidentified | 244 | eligible by default — FLAGGED hole (no ISIN anywhere; see below) |
| rights_entitlement | 217 | excluded (-RE suffix; not a share line) |
| non_equity_name | 43 | excluded (name-pattern fallback) |

Equity-confirmed via `symbol_isin`: **2,850**; via the `EQUITY_L` instrument master: **463** (recent IPOs that carry no ISIN in the cached raw payloads — ETERNAL, GROWW, SWIGGY, HYUNDAI, WAAREEENER, OLAELEC — are resolved here).

**Unidentified instruments (holes):** the build cannot positively confirm 244 store symbols as companies. They default to eligible (the conservative reading for an equity universe) and are FLAGGED, not dropped. Only **2** of them ever reach the top-200 — both are real companies carrying no captured ISIN (an old rename ticker and a recent listing), so no fund contaminates the universe.

- **ICICIMOM30** (gate-b's `unidentified_instrument`): class `equity_unidentified` via `no_isin_no_master`. It is absent from the NSE company master (`EQUITY_L`) — it is an ICICI momentum ETF, not a company — so the instrument master cannot resolve it to equity. It is **named here as a hole**: defaulted eligible but it never ranks top-200 (membership cells: 0). This closes `ca_scope_exclusions.unidentified_instrument`.
- **LIQUIDBEES** (near-constant-NAV cash proxy, must never enter a momentum sort): class `non_equity_isin` — excluded by `INF*` ISIN.

## 6. Rename-Chain Application

`symbol_changes` (1,050 records) links 4,132 store symbols into 3,620 entities; **444** entities span a rename (entity-continuous). Membership is computed per entity, so a rename does not create a spurious exit/entry. Demonstrated continuity — renames where the entity held membership on both the rebalance immediately before and after the effective date:

| Rename | Effective | Member before → after |
|--------|-----------|-----------------------|
| APIL → AIL | 2012-07-11 | `APIL` on 2012-06-29 → `AIL` on 2012-07-31 — member across the rename |
| PANTALOONR → FRL | 2013-04-11 | `PANTALOONR` on 2013-03-28 → `FRL` on 2013-04-30 — member across the rename |
| SESAGOA → SSLT | 2013-10-04 | `SESAGOA` on 2013-09-30 → `SSLT` on 2013-10-31 — member across the rename |
| UNIPHOS → UPL | 2013-10-23 | `UNIPHOS` on 2013-09-30 → `UPL` on 2013-10-31 — member across the rename |

## 7. Overlap with Gate (b) Quarantine (flagged, not dropped)

`ca_scope_exclusions` / `ca_evidence_exceptions` are a **price-adjustment** concern (gate b), not a membership concern: a demerged or disputed-bonus name still existed and traded. These symbols are NOT dropped from the universe; their member-cells are counted here so gate (e) can decide how to treat them.

- Member-cells on `ca_scope_exclusions` symbols: **122**
- Member-cells on `ca_evidence_exceptions` symbols: **8**

Quarantined symbols present in the universe:

| Symbol | Reason |
|--------|--------|
| ABFRL | out_of_scope_corporate_action |
| QUESS | out_of_scope_corporate_action |
| SINTEX | out_of_scope_corporate_action |

## 8. Fit-for-Purpose Statement

- Membership: **35,000** cells across **175** monthly rebalances, every rebalance at exactly 200 members (no shortfalls, no padding).
- Method: `turnover_top200` (mechanical top-200-by-turnover; official PIT history unobtainable — evidenced in §1).
- No-leak truncation test: **PASSED** (16 sampled rebalances byte-identical full-vs-truncated).
- Survivorship: **94** delisted/merged member entities retained for their trading life; no present-day survivor list is a membership input.
- Non-equity exclusion by ISIN (INF*/IN0*/IN9*), name-pattern, and rights-entitlement; ICICIMOM30 named as a hole (absent from the company master, never ranks).
- Gate (b) quarantine overlap flagged, not dropped (122 + 8 member-cells).

**Gate (c) PASSED-eligible.** The universe is point-in-time reconstructable on every rebalance date using only information available at that date, survivorship-bias free (delisted names retained until their last session), entity-continuous across renames, and free of non-equity instruments. The mechanical turnover rule is the charter-locked construct; the official membership history is evidenced-unobtainable, not asserted. Membership is computed from ingested data only; gate (d) (fees) is untouched.
